items.headline: Campbell revises business license tax rates
items.summary: The city council amended the municipal code to update business license tax rates effective in the new fiscal year. This annual adjustment affects local business operating costs and city revenue.

items.headline: Council defers affordable housing subsidy policy to later date
items.summary: The council postponed a decision on how to handle affordable housing restrictions when project-based subsidy contracts expire or properties face foreclosure. This policy affects the city's ability to maintain affordable units long-term.

items.headline: FIFA World Cup 2026: Santa Clara approves giant stadium signs
items.summary: Santa Clara City Council authorized temporary supergraphic signs on buildings near Levi's Stadium during FIFA World Cup 2026 events. This marks a visible change to the city's appearance during the international tournament.

items.headline: Milpitas approves FY 2026-27 development fees and user charges
items.summary: The City Council approved updated development fees and user charges for the fiscal year starting July 1, 2026. These fees affect costs for new construction projects and city services that residents and developers pay.

items.headline: Santa Clara County presents homelessness plan affecting South Bay
items.summary: The County of Santa Clara presented its Community Plan to End Homelessness at the Milpitas council meeting on April 21. This regional initiative could impact local services and policies addressing homelessness in the area.

items.headline: Comm Hill Phase 3.1 housing development approved on Altino Boulevard
items.summary: Four new homes permitted in the Comm Hill Phase 3.1 development along Altino Boulevard, ranging from $487k-$573k. This represents a significant cluster of new single-family residential construction in one of San José's developing neighborhoods.

items.headline: City Council reviews building permit process and development timelines
items.summary: The council discussed a guide outlining San José's process for development projects from initial planning through occupancy, covering permitting requirements and timelines. This could help residents understand how long projects take and what approvals are needed for new construction in the city.

items.headline: Sunnyvale updates climate action plan toward 2028 goals
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission reviewed progress on its Climate Action Playbook and 'Game Plan 2028' during the April 20 meeting. The city is tracking progress on environmental targets through fiscal year 2025-26.
